name: Kusunoki Masashige
description: Kusunoki Masashige was a 14th-century samurai who embodied loyalty and strategic brilliance during the tumultuous Nanboku-chō period of Japanese history. Born in Kawachi Province (modern-day Osaka Prefecture), he rose to prominence as a skilled military tactician and a staunch supporter of Emperor Go-Daigo in his attempt to overthrow the Kamakura shogunate. Kusunoki's unwavering devotion to the Emperor and his innovative defensive strategies, particularly his mastery of guerrilla warfare, earned him widespread admiration and cemented his place as a legendary figure in Japanese folklore. He is celebrated for his selfless sacrifice at the Battle of Minatogawa in 1336, where, facing overwhelming odds, he fought valiantly to delay the advance of Ashikaga Takauji's forces, allowing Emperor Go-Daigo to escape. Kusunoki's death is considered the epitome of samurai loyalty (chūgi) and unwavering dedication to one's lord. His strategic acumen, his profound sense of duty, and his tragic demise have made him a symbol of patriotism and a revered figure in Japanese culture, inspiring generations of warriors and common citizens alike. He is often invoked as an exemplar of selfless service and unwavering commitment to a higher cause, even in the face of certain defeat. Kusunoki’s legacy extends beyond military prowess; he is also remembered as a man of integrity and compassion, deeply concerned for the welfare of the people he served.
subtitle: The Epitome of Samurai Loyalty
achievements: Symbol of samurai loyalty and patriotism

keyMyths.title: Defense of Chihaya Castle
keyMyths.description: Kusunoki Masashige skillfully defended Chihaya Castle against a much larger Kamakura force. He used innovative defensive tactics and guerrilla warfare to hold off the enemy for months. This success significantly weakened the Kamakura shogunate.

keyMyths.title: Battle of Minatogawa
keyMyths.description: Knowing defeat was inevitable, Kusunoki Masashige led his forces against Ashikaga Takauji's army at Minatogawa. He fought bravely, sacrificing himself to buy time for Emperor Go-Daigo to escape. This act cemented his legendary status.

keyMyths.title: Promise of Seven Lives
keyMyths.description: Before his final battle, Kusunoki vowed to be reborn seven times to fight for the Emperor. This demonstrates his unwavering loyalty and commitment to his cause. It is a testament to his dedication.

sources.author: Helen Craig McCullough
sources.work: The Taiheiki: A Chronicle of Medieval Japan
sources.description: An English translation of the Taiheiki, a primary source for the Nanboku-chō period.
sources.text: An English translation of the Taiheiki, a primary source for the Nanboku-chō period.

sources.author: George Sansom
